arts.ttl: Serie A | Juventus v Cagliari: Match Preview
arts.sum: As Serie A action resumes this weekend, Juventus welcomes Cagliari to the Allianz Stadium in Turin for a Sunday lunchtime fixture. The match presents an intriguing contrast between a title-chasing Juventus and a Cagliari side fighting to establish themselves in the top flight. Juventus enters the game in fine form, having secured back-to-back away victories. Most recently, they demonstrated remarkable resilience in a thrilling 3-2 Champions League win over RB Leipzig, overcoming both an early injury to Bremer and a red card for goalkeeper Michele Di Gregorio. This victory followed a convincing 3-0 league triumph against Genoa, pushing the Bianconeri The post Serie A | Juventus v Cagliari: Match Preview appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.ttl: Power Rankings: The best teams in Europe
arts.sum: Real Madrid's shock defeat to LOSC Lille and Bayern Munich's equally shocking 1-0 defeat to Aston Villa grabbed most of the column space, while some was also saved for Juventus's dramatic win over RB Leipzig too. Borussia Dortmund earn a spot in the rankings this week thanks to their thumping 7-1 win over an in-form Celtic side in the Champions League in midweek.

arts.ttl: Pogba's Juventus future set to end as new venture revealed
arts.sum: Paul Pogba will not be playing for Juventus again as his contract will either be terminated or a mutual agreement for separation will be reached, according to reports. Reporter Nicolo Schira tweeted on Friday: "Paul #Pogba is not in #Juventus’ plans: he won’t return to play for #Juve.

arts.ttl: Juventus Eye January Swoop for Unsettled Barcelona Forward
arts.sum: After a proactive summer transfer window, Juventus are back to the drawing board, plotting strategies for the upcoming transfer window. According to Spanish news outlet Mundo Deportivo, Barcelona winger Ferran Torres has landed on Bianconeri's radar amid Cristiano Giuntoli's effort to sign a dependable alternative for Dusan Vlahovic.
